subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Distinguish between metals and non-metals based on physical properties
Identify common metals and non-metals used in daily life
Understand basic chemical properties like reaction with oxygen and water
Recognize the concept of the reactivity series in simple terms
Understand what alloys are and why they are useful
Identify common methods to prevent rusting

lesson_plan.title: Physical Properties of Metals
teaching_strategy: Show and Tell: Displaying copper wire, aluminum foil, and iron nails
Simple experiment: Testing conductivity with a battery and bulb
Hammering a piece of coal vs. an aluminum wire
student_activities: List 5 metals found in the classroom
Classify materials based on 'shiny' or 'dull' appearance
assessment: Defining terms like Malleability and Ductility

lesson_plan.title: Physical Properties of Non-Metals
topics_covered: Poor conductors of heat and electricity
teaching_strategy: Comparing a piece of sulfur/carbon with an iron rod
Discussion on exceptions: Why is Mercury liquid? Why does Graphite conduct?
Visual chart of non-metal states (solid, liquid, gas)
student_activities: Draw a table comparing metals and non-metals
Identify the non-metal used in pencil leads
assessment: Identifying exceptions in a quick-fire round

lesson_plan.title: Chemical Properties: Reaction with Oxygen
teaching_strategy: Demonstration: Burning Magnesium ribbon and testing ash with litmus
Simple word equations
Visual aids showing rusted iron vs. tarnished silver
student_activities: Observe the color change of litmus in Magnesium Oxide solution
Write the word equation for: Magnesium + Oxygen
assessment: Short answer: What is the nature of Magnesium Oxide?

lesson_plan.title: Reaction with Water and Acids
topics_covered: Reaction of metals with dilute acids
teaching_strategy: Video clip of Sodium reacting with water (Safety first!)
Lab demo: Iron/Zinc with dilute $HCl$
The 'Pop' sound experiment
student_activities: List metals that react fast vs. metals that don't react with water
assessment: Fill in the blanks: Metal + Acid → Salt + _____
Exit slip: Which gas is released when metals react with acids?

lesson_plan.title: The Reactivity Series
teaching_strategy: Story analogy: A stronger person pushing a weaker person off a chair
Demonstration: Iron nail in Copper Sulphate solution
Mnemonic to remember the series (e.g., 'Please Stop Calling Me...')
student_activities: Observe the color change from blue to green in $CuSO_{4}$
Arrange 4 metals in order of reactivity based on a chart
assessment: Matching activity: Metal to its reactivity level
Predicting the outcome of a simple displacement

lesson_plan.title: Corrosion and Alloys
teaching_strategy: Showing rusted vs. galvanized nails
Discussion on why stainless steel doesn't rust
Displaying brass items or bronze medals
student_activities: Create a poster on 'How to Save My Bicycle from Rusting'
Identify the metals present in Brass and Steel

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Assessment
teaching_strategy: Interactive Quiz (Jeopardy style)
Mind-map drawing on the board
Doubt clearing session

teaching_aids: Samples: Fe, Cu, Al, S, C (Coal/Graphite)
Battery, bulb, and wires for conductivity test
Iron nails and Copper Sulphate solution
teacher_tip: Focus heavily on physical properties and real-life examples. Using a 'Mnemonic' for the reactivity series at this early stage helps students feel more confident about a topic that usually seems difficult.
